actual_current_surface_status: exact negative boundary / same-1PI not PR230 closure
proposal_allowed_reason: The same-1PI route does not independently fix scalar LSZ/pole normalization.
required_next_theorem: derive scalar pole residue/canonical normalization independently of H_unit matrix elements
then use any same-1PI coefficient only after y and D_phi are separately normalized
strict_non_claims: does not reject the same-1PI g_bare support route in its own scope
does not define y_t through H_unit
does not use observed top mass or Yukawa values
verdict: The existing same-1PI route is not a hidden PR #230 top-Yukawa closure.  Its current notes use H_unit/Rep-B matrix-element data and are audited conditional.  More basically, a four-fermion coefficient fixes y^2 D_phi, not the separately normalized physical scalar vertex y and scalar pole residue D_phi.  A scalar pole/LSZ normalization theorem is still required.
